description_wiki: Okayama (岡山市, Okayama-shi; Japanese: [okaꜜjama]) is the capital city of Okayama Prefecture in the Chūgoku region of Japan.

description: Founder of Nichiren Buddhism in Japan
description_wiki: Nichiren (日蓮; Japanese pronunciation: [ɲi.tɕiꜜ.ɾeɴ, ɲiꜜ.tɕi.ɾeɴ], 16 February 1222 – 13 October 1282) was a Japanese Buddhist monk and philosopher of the Kamakura period.

description: Abrahamic monotheistic religion founded on the teachings, person and life of Jesus
description_wiki: Christianity is an Abrahamic monotheistic religion, which states that Jesus is the Son of God and rose from the dead after his crucifixion, whose coming as the messiah (Christ) was prophesied in the Old Testament and chronicled in the New Testament.

description: private Catholic Jesuit university in Spokane, Washington, US
description_wiki: Gonzaga University (GU) ( ghən-ZAG-ə) is a private Jesuit university in Spokane, Washington, United States.

description: city in and county seat of Hood River County, Oregon, United States
description_wiki: Hood River is a city in and the county seat of Hood River County, Oregon, United States.

The Tule Lake War Relocation Center, also known as the Tule Lake Segregation Center, was an American concentration camp located in Modoc and Siskiyou counties in California and constructed in 1942 by the United States government to incarcerate Japanese Americans, forcibly removing them from their homes on the West Coast of the United States.

description: city in and county seat of Spokane County, Washington, United States
description_wiki: Spokane (  spoh-KAN; Spokane: sƛ̓x̌etkʷ) is a city in and the county seat of Spokane County in the U.S. state of Washington.

description: capital city of the United States of America
description_wiki: Washington, D.C., officially the District of Columbia and commonly known as simply Washington or D.C., is the capital city and federal district of the United States.

aliases: Ground forces of the United States Armed Forces
description: land service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Army (U.S. Army) is the land service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

description: 1941 Japanese surprise attack on the U.S
description_wiki: On December 7, 1941, the Empire of Japan launched a surprise military strike on the United States Pacific Fleet at its naval base at Pearl Harbor on Oahu, Hawaii.

entity: Atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki
surface_forms: America dropped A-bombs on Hiroshima and Nagasaki
description: 1945 use of nuclear weapons against Japan in World War II
description_wiki: On 6 and 9 August 1945, the United States detonated two atomic bombs over the Japanese cities of Hiroshima and Nagasaki, respectively, during the final days of World War II. The aerial bombings killed 150,000 to 246,000 people, most of whom were civilians, and remain the first and only uses of nuclear weapons in an armed conflict.

description: village in Sula Islands Regency, North Maluku, Indonesia
state of the United States of America
description_wiki: California () is a state in the Western United States that lies on the Pacific Coast.

description: city in and county seat of King County, State of Washington, United States
description_wiki: Seattle (  see-AT-əl) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Washington and the Pacific Northwest region of North America.
Harrington is a city in Lincoln County, Washington, United States.

description: city in Snohomish County, Washington, United States
description_wiki: Monroe is a city in Snohomish County, Washington, United States.
The Great Depression was a severe global economic downturn from 1929 to 1939.

description: world War II Japanese troop transport
description_wiki: Hawaii Maru was a 9,482-ton Japanese troop transport during World War II, which sank on 2 December 1944 with great loss of life.
